label: Diffusion & score-based models

node.label: Diffusion & score-based models
node.definition: Diffusion and score-based models learn to reverse a staged corruption process, synthesizing samples by iterative denoising or integration of a learned score field.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Diffusion & score-based models is modeled as depending on Denoising diffusion & stochastic corruption, Score matching & denoising score objectives and feeding downstream nodes such as Image generation, editing & design, Video generation & simulation synthesis, Speech synthesis, voice & music generation.
examples: atlas downstream link to Image generation, editing & design
atlas downstream link to Video generation & simulation synthesis
atlas downstream link to Speech synthesis, voice & music generation
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Denoising diffusion & stochastic corruption, Score matching & denoising score objectives.

dependencies.rationale: Diffusion & score-based models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Denoising diffusion & stochastic corruption.

In this atlas graph, Diffusion & score-based models has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Score matching & denoising score objectives because Score matching & denoising score objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
In this atlas graph, Diffusion & score-based models has a `specializes` dependency on U-Nets & encoder–decoder convnets with `common` strength.
In this atlas graph, Diffusion & score-based models has a `composes` dependency on Reparameterization & differentiable sampling with `common` strength.
